title: Naturalistic viewing: An open-access dataset using simultaneous EEG-fMRI
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous EEG-fMRI recordings from 22 participants (ages 23-51) during naturalistic viewing, collected at the Nathan Kline Institute. The 64-channel EEG data (61 cortical channels, 2 EOG, 1 ECG) is paired with fMRI acquisitions, supplemented by eye-tracking and respiratory recordings. The dataset aims to elucidate the relationship between electrical brain activity and hemodynamic fluctuations during naturalistic stimulation.
methods_description: Data were collected using simultaneous EEG-fMRI acquisition with a customized Brain Products BrainCapMR 64-channel system. Eye tracking was performed using EyeLink 1000 inside the scanner. Physiological monitoring included respiratory recordings via BIOPAC MP150. Participants completed behavioral assessments including the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index, sleep duration, and caffeine intake prior to scanning.
